summary.intent: Migrate the web UI from plain JavaScript with HTM tagged templates to TypeScript + JSX/TSX with Vite bundling, then incrementally improve type safety with enums, typed RPC/WebSocket layers, and reusable Preact components.
summary.outcome: Full TypeScript migration completed on branch feat/typescript-migration: 135 JS files → 142 TS/TSX files, Vite build pipeline established, typed RPC (107 methods), typed WebSocket events (28-member enum), ChannelType enum, shared component library (18 components + 2 hooks), all large files split under 1500 lines, local-validate passing clean. Branch rebased onto main with Matrix OIDC changes ported.

repo: The web UI uses Preact with HTM tagged templates (no JSX/bundler) served as native ES modules — a zero-build-step architecture intentionally chosen for fast dev iteration
Rust embeds JS/CSS via include_dir! macro; dist/ must be committed so cargo build works without Node.js installed
The codebase uses a registerPage + raw DOM imperative pattern alongside Preact components — mixed architecture that limits type safety gains
local-validate.sh runs checks in parallel; it auto-detects PR from gh pr view and can fail if an unrelated PR is detected on an unpushed branch
File size limit (1500 lines) in check-file-size.sh previously only checked .rs files — TS/TSX was not covered until this session
git add -A accidentally picks up unrelated working-tree changes from other sessions — stray Rust files from other branches were committed and had to be reverted
Biome cannot lint inside HTM tagged template literals — a11y violations in old code were invisible until migration to JSX made them visible
Locale files (en/fr/zh, 54 files) use plain export default {} objects — Node.js dynamic import() works on .ts files that contain only object literals
GPG/YubiKey signing requires physical tap for each commit — background/automated commits will block waiting for PIN entry
Vite build generates source maps by default adding 27MB to dist/ — must be disabled in vite.config.ts for embedded binary use
Subagents occasionally hallucinate imports (sendChat imported from ChatPage into websocket.ts) — cross-file call relationships must be verified against originals
The rebase of this branch produced repeated dist/ file conflicts for every commit — resolving with --ours and rebuilding at the end is the right strategy

code.finding: RpcMethodMap maps 107 RPC method name strings to their request/response types — sendRpc<'models.list'>() infers response shape at compile time
WsEventName enum (28 members) + WsEventPayloadMap discriminated union — onEvent(WsEventName.Chat, cb) gives cb a typed ChatPayload automatically
ChannelType enum replaces ~60 string literal comparisons ('telegram', 'discord', etc.)
S.$<T>() generic DOM accessor eliminates most HTMLElement→specific-type casts at call sites
Barrel export for 18 shared form/UI components — TextField, SelectField, TabBar, Modal, Badge, EmptyState, Loading, useSaveState, useRpc, etc.
useRpc hook encapsulates useEffect + sendRpc + loading/error state pattern repeated in every page
Pre-refactor: ~280 lines of imperative createElement dialogs duplicating the Preact Modal component above them — replaced with JSX components
sourcemap: false required to avoid 27MB of .map files being committed and embedded in the Rust binary via include_dir!
shiki_url template variable and DEFAULT_SHIKI_CDN_URL removed — Vite bundles shiki at build time eliminating the CDN dependency and CSP allowlist entry
Extended to check .ts/.tsx files in addition to .rs — previously only Rust was covered
Added tsc --noEmit check running in parallel with biome — biome handles formatting/lint rules but does not do type checking

workflow: Biome handles formatting + lint rules but NOT TypeScript type checking — both biome and tsc --noEmit are needed in CI
Subagents are effective for parallel file conversion but hallucinate imports and use wrong directory assumptions — always verify cross-file dependencies
For large file conversions (5000+ lines), subagents hit output token limits — must split into part 1/part 2 subagents
After rebase with many conflicts, rebuild dist/ fresh rather than resolving individual generated file conflicts
git add -A is dangerous in a mixed workspace — stage specific paths to avoid picking up unrelated changes from other in-progress work
TypeScript strict mode with 0 errors is achievable via as-casts initially, but as-casts are runtime-invisible — replace with type guards and proper interfaces iteratively
Splitting large files (>1500 lines) is mechanical but requires updating all import paths — a subagent can do this but needs the full dependency graph
Extracting shared components from repeated JSX patterns requires reading 3-4 files first to identify the canonical pattern before writing the abstraction

friction: YubiKey/GPG signing blocked every commit during autonomous operation — the session had to pause multiple times for physical taps
git add -A in early commits accidentally included unrelated Rust working-tree changes (auth_routes/vault.rs, public_origin.rs) causing cargo check failures that had to be diagnosed and reverted
Subagents hit output token limits on 5000+ line files (SettingsPage 6855 lines, providers 2325) — required manual split into part1/part2 subagents
Rebase produced repeated dist/ file conflicts on every commit — no clean way to skip generated file conflicts during interactive rebase
Biome a11y rules (useButtonType, noLabelWithoutControl) became visible for the first time after JSX migration — 289 new violations that were pre-existing but invisible in HTM templates
TypeScript strict mode produced 236 errors after mechanical migration — required a dedicated subagent pass to fix with proper narrowing vs. as-casts
Background task notifications arrived after the main conversation had already fixed the issues — caused confusion about whether current state was clean
The sendChat cross-module reference in websocket.ts was hallucinated by a subagent — required reading original JS to determine it was never imported, was a pre-existing loose reference
npx --prefix does not set working directory for tsc — the local-validate.sh script needed cd crates/web/ui && npx tsc pattern instead
Rate limits hit twice during the autonomous migration pass ('You've hit your limit') requiring user to resume the session
open_items: 234 remaining as HTML*Element casts from querySelector/closest/createElement — eliminating these requires migrating imperative registerPage pages to Preact component trees (own PR)
32 Record<string, unknown> in genuinely dynamic contexts (channel configs, ws frames, gon data) — need concrete interface definitions as each feature is worked on
Provider modals system (~2200 lines of imperative createElement in providers/) — full Preact component rewrite deferred to own PR
464 inline style objects — should be replaced with Tailwind classes but is a CSS cleanup pass, not a component extraction
Refine unknown placeholder types in RpcMethodMap — 107 methods typed but response types default to unknown until individual endpoints are refined

SettingsPage split into 12 section files — remaining sections (SecuritySection, SshSection, ConfigSection, RemoteAccessSection, VoiceSection) not yet converted to shared form components due to complex custom layouts
E2E tests (38 files, ~11K LoC) not migrated to TypeScript — low value but creates inconsistency
useRpcAll variant or multi-fetch hook not built — pages with multiple concurrent RPC calls still use manual useEffect chains
dist/ is committed to git — long-term this means every UI change adds binary diffs; consider a cargo build.rs script that runs npm build instead
Delete old JS source (crates/web/src/assets/js/) was done but share-app.mjs and share-theme-init.js remain as vanilla JS — share page not yet migrated to the Vite pipeline
